note: Location benefits/drawbacks to skills (Erik ratified 2026-07-06). TWO layers. (1) VECTOR ALIGNMENT (mechanic, resolution-math): an ability whose axes align with a location's STRONG spectrum axes eases the roll; opposed axes harden it — small, capped bumps, engine-computed from location.spectrum vs ability.axes. (2) TYPE AFFINITY (this file, content): a location TAG grants a flat situational bump to listed skills/attributes. Both surface to the player (LLW: show why a roll was easier here). Bumps small so place colors play, never dominates it. Aevi authors type-affinity content; vector-alignment shape is Erik's (resolution-math) — draft curve below for his ratify.

vectorAlignment.draftCurve: for each of a location's strong axes (|v| >= 0.4), if the ability's axis sign matches, +ease; if opposite, +hardness. Cap total location modifier at +/-10 on the d100 chance. Perceived only after the player knows the vectors (SNG-011 vectorsKnown) — otherwise applied silently and revealed as 'the place favored this'.
vectorAlignment.ratified: Erik 2026-07-06 — bias in general ratified; certain areas amplified beyond the baseline

amplitude.note: Baseline vector cap is +/-10. A location's amplitude MULTIPLIES its cap so charged places swing wider. Derived default from spectrum charge (sum of |axis| over strong axes): ordinary ~1.0, tilted ~1.5, extreme ~2.0. Explicit per-location/per-tag override wins. Aevi authors the designations.

forge.note: workshops, smithies — making and mending come easier

shrine.note: sacred/quiet places resist the foreclosing tools

market.note: trade and crowds reward appraisal and negotiation

wild.note: wilderness rewards trackers, punishes parlor skills

ruin.note: old construction reads itself to the right eye

precursor.note: live remnant sites amplify Precursor craft — and unsettle the spirit

settled.note: towns and halls reward the social and the memory-keeping crafts

high.note: heights and open terraces — far sight helps, hiding does not
